name: The Lost Pyramid
description: A sandstorm has scoured away three thousand years of silence. The pyramid stands exposed against the sky, its entrance gaping like a wound in the earth. Hieroglyphics flanking the threshold promise two things: treasure beyond imagining, and death for any who seek it. You take the torch. You step inside. Behind you, the sand shifts and seals the way out. The desert has swallowed your retreat. Forward, then. Into the dark.
synopsis: Brave ancient traps inside a buried pyramid.

entrance.name: Pyramid Entrance
entrance.description: Limestone blocks the size of chariots frame a passage descending into blackness. Carved cobras flank the doorway, their hooded heads worn smooth by centuries of wind. Each step kicks up golden dust that hangs in your torchlight like suspended time. The air tastes of mineral and age — dry as bone, undisturbed for millennia. Hieroglyphics crowd every surface, their faded pigments whispering of a pharaoh who conquered death itself. A grand corridor yawns to the north. To the east, a narrow crack in the wall breathes cool air — a hidden passage.

hazards.description: Fine sand trickles from cracks overhead. Somewhere deep in the stone, something groans and shifts.

grand-corridor.name: The Grand Corridor
grand-corridor.description: Painted gods process along the walls in vivid silence — jackal-headed Anubis weighing a feather against a human heart, golden barques sailing a river of stars, armies of the dead marching toward eternity. The pigments still burn with color after three millennia. Oil channels cut into the floor once held ribbons of flame; now they cradle only dust. The ceiling vaults high overhead, supported by pillars carved as bundled papyrus stalks. The entrance lies south. Passages branch west toward an offering hall and east toward a chamber dominated by a carved sphinx. A sealed stone door to the north bears a scarab-shaped indentation.
grand-corridor.solvedDescription: Painted gods process along the walls in vivid silence — jackal-headed Anubis weighing a feather against a human heart, golden barques sailing a river of stars, armies of the dead marching toward eternity. The pigments still burn with color after three millennia. Oil channels cut into the floor once held ribbons of flame; now they cradle only dust. The ceiling vaults high overhead, supported by pillars carved as bundled papyrus stalks. The entrance lies south. Passages branch west toward an offering hall and east toward a chamber dominated by a carved sphinx. The massive stone door to the north has ground aside — the scarab set in its indentation unlocking ancient counterweights — revealing the burial chamber beyond.

hazards.description: Tiny holes line the walls at ankle height — dart traps, ancient but possibly still armed. The mechanisms gleam with oil that never dried.

hidden-passage.name: Hidden Passage
hidden-passage.description: The walls press in. Rough-hewn stone scrapes your shoulders as you shuffle through a tunnel never meant for the living. Cobwebs thick as gauze cling to your face and hair. The chisel marks are hasty, panicked — this passage was carved in secret, outside the master plan. Scratched into the wall in hieratic script, barely legible: 'FOR THE KEEPER ALONE.' The passage twists west to the entrance. To the north, it opens into a wider chamber.
hidden-passage.solvedDescription: The walls press in. Rough-hewn stone scrapes your shoulders as you shuffle through a tunnel never meant for the living. Cobwebs thick as gauze cling to your face and hair. The chisel marks are hasty, panicked — this passage was carved in secret, outside the master plan. Scratched into the wall in hieratic script, barely legible: 'FOR THE KEEPER ALONE.' The passage twists west to the entrance. To the north, it opens into the trap room — but the ancient scroll has revealed the safe path, rendering the pressure plate mechanism harmless for those who know the way.

offering-hall.name: The Offering Hall
offering-hall.description: The smell hits first — ancient grain turned to powder, dried resin, the faint sweetness of honey still perfectly preserved. Stone shelves line the walls, bearing mummified offerings: bread collapsed to dust, fruit shrunken into dark husks, alabaster jars sealed tight against eternity. A black granite altar dominates the center, its surface carved with prayers to Osiris and stained dark with ancient libations. Behind it, canopic jars painted with the four sons of Horus stand in solemn procession. The grand corridor lies east. A passage north leads deeper into the pyramid's heart.

hazards.description: The air here is thick and stale, pressing against your lungs like wet cloth. Your torch flame shrinks to a blue whisper.

sphinx-chamber.name: The Sphinx Chamber
sphinx-chamber.description: A great sphinx crouches against the far wall, twice your height, its human face worn smooth by time into something both regal and unknowable. Between its outstretched paws, a stone pedestal holds a shallow bowl-shaped depression. Riddles in demotic script spiral across the walls — questions without answers, answers without questions. Moonlight slants down through a shaft in the ceiling, impossibly, painting the sphinx's face in cold silver. The grand corridor lies west. To the north, a heavy door carved with the Eye of Horus bars the way forward.
sphinx-chamber.solvedDescription: A great sphinx crouches against the far wall, twice your height, its human face worn smooth by time into something both regal and unknowable. Between its outstretched paws, the stone pedestal's shallow bowl now holds the sun disc, blazing where moonlight strikes it from above. Riddles in demotic script spiral across the walls — questions without answers, answers without questions. Moonlight slants down through a shaft in the ceiling, impossibly, painting the sphinx's face in cold silver. The grand corridor lies west. To the north, the heavy door carved with the Eye of Horus has split apart and ground open, granting passage to the trap room beyond.

trap-room.name: The Trap Room
trap-room.description: A mosaic of pressure plates tessellates the floor, each tile engraved with a different hieroglyph. Scorch marks blacken the walls. Scattered bones tell the rest of the story. Spring-loaded mechanisms line both sides — some spent, their bronze teeth bared, others still coiled tight and hungry. In the far corner, a skeleton in sun-bleached khakis lies crumpled, one hand outstretched toward something that glints gold. The hidden passage retreats south. A doorway to the west is sealed by a collapsed stone slab.

hazards.description: Pressure plates cover the floor in an unbroken mosaic. Each step could be your last — the mechanisms are still tensed to strike.

burial-chamber.name: The Burial Chamber
burial-chamber.description: Stone sarcophagi ring the walls — servants, priests, nobles, all entombed to attend the pharaoh through eternity. Their painted faces stare with flat, patient eyes. Gold leaf curls from the ceiling in delicate spirals, drifting down like metallic snow. A low hum vibrates through the floor and into your bones. The pyramid is breathing in its sleep. The grand corridor lies south. To the east, an ornate doorway framed in lapis lazuli leads to the pharaoh's inner sanctum — but its mechanism demands an offering before it will open.
burial-chamber.solvedDescription: Stone sarcophagi ring the walls — servants, priests, nobles, all entombed to attend the pharaoh through eternity. Their painted faces stare with flat, patient eyes. Gold leaf curls from the ceiling in delicate spirals, drifting down like metallic snow. A low hum vibrates through the floor and into your bones. The pyramid is breathing in its sleep. The grand corridor lies south. The ornate doorway to the east, framed in lapis lazuli, has swung open — the golden ankh having unlocked its elaborate mechanism — revealing the pharaoh's inner sanctum beyond, glowing with impossible golden light.

hazards.description: A faint green mist clings to the floor, curling around the sarcophagi like searching fingers. The pharaoh's curse, given form.

treasure-room.name: The Treasure Room
treasure-room.description: Gold. Your torch catches it and the chamber detonates into blinding radiance. Golden cats, golden falcons, jeweled collars heaped on ivory game boards, chariots sheathed in electrum, chests hemorrhaging lapis lazuli and carnelian. A nation's ransom, untouched for three thousand years. The air itself tastes of metal. But the walls speak a warning, carved deep into the stone: 'TAKE NOTHING. THE WEIGHT OF GREED WILL CRUSH YOU.' The offering hall lies south. To the east, a passage leads toward something that smells of open sky.

hazards.description: A single cracked pillar supports the ceiling. The weight of a million limestone blocks presses down. The crack is wider than it was a moment ago.

pharaohs-tomb.name: The Pharaoh's Tomb
pharaohs-tomb.description: The air turns cold. A single sarcophagus of black basalt dominates the chamber — massive, absolute. Its lid is carved in the perfect likeness of Pharaoh Khetankhamen: serene, eternal, terrible. The walls are solid gold, every inch engraved with the complete Book of the Dead — spells to carry a soul through the underworld and into the stars. Four Anubis statues stand sentinel at the corners, obsidian eyes catching your torchlight with an almost-living gleam. The sarcophagus lid sits slightly ajar. Something breathes in the dark beneath it. The burial chamber lies west.

star-chamber.name: The Star Chamber
star-chamber.description: The ceiling opens to the sky. A narrow shaft, cut with impossible precision, frames the North Star in its aperture — the ancient builders' final triumph of alignment. Star charts carved into the obsidian floor map constellations three thousand years dead, charting the soul's passage to the Field of Reeds. Lenses of polished crystal focus the starlight into a single beam that strikes a golden disc on the south wall. The disc can be rotated. The treasure room lies west. Something hums at the edge of hearing — the stars themselves, perhaps, singing the dead home.
star-chamber.solvedDescription: The ceiling opens to the sky. A narrow shaft, cut with impossible precision, frames the North Star in its aperture — the ancient builders' final triumph of alignment. Star charts carved into the obsidian floor map constellations three thousand years dead, charting the soul's passage to the Field of Reeds. Lenses of polished crystal focus the starlight into a single beam that strikes the golden disc on the south wall. The disc has been rotated, and the Eye of Horus placed in its slot — projecting constellations in cold fire across the floor. Where the beams converge, a hidden compartment has split open in the obsidian, revealing the Scepter of Ra, golden and radiant, humming with the authority of a god. The treasure room lies west.

torch.name: Torch
torch.description: A pitch-soaked reed torch that burns with a steady amber flame. It spits and crackles softly, pushing back the darkness that has ruled these corridors for thirty centuries.
torch.pickupText: You lift the torch from its bracket. Shadows reel across the walls like startled things.
torch.roomText: A torch gutters in a bronze bracket beside the entrance, its flame stubborn against the dark.

ancient-scroll.name: Ancient Scroll
ancient-scroll.description: Brittle papyrus sealed with wax bearing the cartouche of Pharaoh Khetankhamen. Unrolled, it reveals a map of the pyramid's traps — and a riddle inked in careful hieratic: 'The sphinx drinks moonlight; feed it the sun.'
ancient-scroll.pickupText: You ease the scroll from its hiding place. The papyrus crackles like old bones, but holds.
ancient-scroll.roomText: A rolled papyrus scroll juts from a crack in the passage wall, its wax seal crumbling.

scarab-amulet.name: Scarab Amulet
scarab-amulet.description: A carved lapis lazuli scarab set in hammered gold. Sacred to Khepri, god of the rising sun. It radiates faint warmth, as though it still remembers the desert heat it was carved beneath.
scarab-amulet.pickupText: You lift the scarab from the pedestal. Warmth pulses against your palm — a tiny heartbeat of trapped sunlight.
scarab-amulet.roomText: A brilliant blue scarab amulet rests on the pedestal between the sphinx's paws, catching the moonlight.

golden-ankh.name: Golden Ankh
golden-ankh.description: The key of life, cast in solid gold with turquoise veins. Heavier than it looks — the weight of divine authority made tangible. The loop at the top fits perfectly in the hand, as if it were made for holding.
golden-ankh.pickupText: You lift the ankh from the altar. A low hum resonates through the gold and into your wrist. It feels alive.
golden-ankh.roomText: A golden ankh gleams on the altar, its turquoise inlay vivid against the dark stone.

canopic-jar.name: Alabaster Canopic Jar
canopic-jar.description: A beautifully carved canopic jar of white alabaster, its lid shaped like a jackal's head. Something shifts inside when you tilt it.
canopic-jar.roomText: An alabaster canopic jar stands upright near the sarcophagus, its jackal-head lid perfectly intact, untouched by the green mist that curls around its base.
canopic-jar.deathText: You lift the jar's lid and the green mist surges upward, pouring from the jar like a living thing. It rises — ankle, waist, throat. It constricts. Ancient preservative gases, concentrated for millennia, fill your lungs with the taste of eternity. You become another relic of the tomb.

pharaohs-mask.name: Pharaoh's Death Mask
pharaohs-mask.description: A death mask of beaten gold with lapis lazuli eyes and a striped nemes headdress. The face is serene, ageless — the idealized likeness of Pharaoh Khetankhamen, frozen between life and eternity.
pharaohs-mask.pickupText: You pry the mask from the skeleton's outstretched fingers. The golden face regards you with unsettling calm, as if it has been waiting.
pharaohs-mask.roomText: A golden death mask glints near the outstretched hand of a fallen explorer, half-buried in dust.

eye-of-horus.name: Eye of Horus
eye-of-horus.description: The wedjat — the Eye of Horus — carved from a single piece of obsidian so black it seems to drink the light. Said to ward against evil and grant true sight in the deepest dark.
eye-of-horus.pickupText: You take the Eye. The obsidian is ice-cold, and for a heartbeat the corridor blazes with color — painted, lit, alive, as it was three thousand years ago. Then the vision fades.
eye-of-horus.roomText: An obsidian Eye of Horus hangs from a bronze peg, swaying almost imperceptibly, as if someone just touched it.

sun-disc.name: Sun Disc
sun-disc.description: A polished golden disc engraved with the face of Ra. Even in dim light it blazes — a miniature sun forged by human hands, focusing and magnifying every stray photon that strikes its surface.
sun-disc.pickupText: You wrench the disc from its mount. Torchlight floods its surface and the chamber flares white for an instant. You hold captured sunlight in your hands.
sun-disc.roomText: A golden disc engraved with Ra's face is mounted on the south wall, blazing where the starlight strikes it.

scepter-of-ra.name: Scepter of Ra
scepter-of-ra.description: A golden scepter crowned with a miniature sun disc, encrusted with rubies and turquoise. The was-scepter — symbol of divine authority over Upper and Lower Egypt. It thrums in your grip, warm and restless, as if the power of Ra himself strains against the gold.
scepter-of-ra.pickupText: You lift the Scepter of Ra from its compartment. Golden light floods the chamber, and for a moment you cast no shadow. You hold the authority of a god.
scepter-of-ra.roomText: The Scepter of Ra burns with golden light in a hidden compartment in the floor, impossible to ignore.

scarab-pendant.name: Golden Scarab Pendant
scarab-pendant.description: A heavy gold pendant shaped like a scarab beetle, its wings inlaid with lapis lazuli. It feels warm, as though sunlight is trapped inside.
scarab-pendant.roomText: A golden scarab pendant lies half-buried in the sand that trickles from above, glinting like a trapped sunrise.
scarab-pendant.deathText: You brush sand from the scarab and the ceiling groans. A massive limestone block wrenches free and descends with the slow inevitability of a closing tomb. There is no time to run. Three thousand years of patience, and the pyramid claims another offering.

jade-figurine.name: Jade Cat Figurine
jade-figurine.description: A small jade figurine of a seated cat, its emerald eyes eerily lifelike. Bastet, perhaps, watching over the dead.
jade-figurine.roomText: A jade figurine of a cat sits in a small alcove between the dart holes, its emerald eyes watching the corridor with serene indifference.
jade-figurine.deathText: You reach into the alcove for the figurine. A click beneath your hand. A volley of darts erupts from the walls — bronze needles black with three-thousand-year-old poison. They find flesh before you find the floor.

incense-censer.name: Golden Censer
incense-censer.description: An ornate golden censer on a fine chain, its bowl still holding crumbled incense that smells faintly of myrrh and something sweeter.
incense-censer.roomText: A golden censer dangles from a hook near the altar, its chain still bright, a faint scent of ancient myrrh drifting from its bowl.
incense-censer.deathText: You lift the censer and the last of the ancient incense crumbles to dust. Three thousand years of sealed decay release their poison in a single breath. Your lungs fill with air that was old when Rome was young. You cannot cough. You cannot breathe. You join the offerings.

jeweled-ankh.name: Jeweled Ankh
jeweled-ankh.description: An ankh cross of polished obsidian, its loop set with a single ruby that pulses like a heartbeat in certain light.
jeweled-ankh.roomText: A jeweled ankh rests on a raised tile in the center of the room, its ruby heart pulsing faintly in the torchlight.
jeweled-ankh.deathText: You lift the ankh from its tile. Stone screams against stone. The walls lurch inward with grinding, mechanical fury — ancient gears awakened by the missing weight. The ceiling descends. The ankh's ruby pulses once more, the last light you'll ever see.

golden-lotus.name: Golden Lotus Flower
golden-lotus.description: A lotus blossom wrought in pure gold, each petal paper-thin and impossibly detailed. Dewdrops of crystal cling to its petals.
golden-lotus.roomText: A golden lotus flower sits atop a pile of lesser treasures, its crystal dewdrops scattering tiny rainbows across the chamber walls.
golden-lotus.deathText: You pluck the golden lotus from its perch. Something shifts. The cracked pillar groans, surrenders. The ceiling descends with the slow inevitability of geological time, a million limestone blocks reclaiming their rest. The lotus gleams in your hand as the darkness closes in.

open-burial-chamber.description: A sealed stone door blocks the way north. At its center, a scarab-shaped indentation is carved with exacting precision. No amount of force will move this door — it was built to outlast empires.
open-burial-chamber.hintText: The indentation is shaped precisely like a scarab. Somewhere in this pyramid, there must be an amulet that fits.
open-burial-chamber.solvedText: You press the scarab amulet into the indentation. A perfect fit. Deep within the walls, ancient gears grind to life — stone screaming against stone — and the massive door rises, revealing the burial chamber beyond. Dust cascades through the widening gap like a curtain of gold.

open-pharaohs-tomb.description: An ornate doorway to the east is sealed by an elaborate mechanism. At its center, a slot is carved in the unmistakable shape of an ankh — the Egyptian key of life.
open-pharaohs-tomb.hintText: The slot is shaped like an ankh. Only a key of life — and one worthy of a pharaoh — could open this door.
open-pharaohs-tomb.solvedText: The golden ankh slides into the slot as if the mechanism were forged around it. Counterweights shift within the walls with a deep, resonant click. The ornate door swings inward, exhaling air that hasn't moved in three millennia. Beyond, the pharaoh's inner tomb glows with impossible golden light.

clear-trap-room.description: The passage north opens into a room where pressure plates tessellate the floor. Scorch marks and scattered bones beyond the threshold tell you everything. Blundering through means death.
clear-trap-room.hintText: The ancient scroll maps every trap in this pyramid. Reading it before entering that room could save your life.
clear-trap-room.solvedText: You unroll the scroll and trace the trap diagram with your finger. The safe path emerges: third tile, first, fourth, first, fifth. You commit it to memory, breathing slow. The trap room is navigable now — if your nerve holds.

action.hazard: Pressure plates cover the floor in an unbroken mosaic. Each step could be your last — the mechanisms are still tensed to strike.

unlock-sphinx-door.description: A heavy door to the north is carved with the Eye of Horus. At its center, a circular depression is ringed with engraved sun rays — shaped to cradle a disc.
unlock-sphinx-door.hintText: The depression radiates carved sunbeams. A disc bearing the face of the sun god might complete the pattern.
unlock-sphinx-door.solvedText: You seat the sun disc into the depression. It locks home with a resonant click, and the Eye of Horus splits down the center as the stone door grinds open. Warm light floods through from the chamber beyond. The trap room lies to the north.

reveal-star-treasure.description: The golden disc on the wall can be rotated to redirect the beam of focused starlight. Beside it, a slot is carved in the shape of an eye — perhaps one with the power to see what the stars are truly showing.
reveal-star-treasure.hintText: The slot is shaped like an eye. The Eye of Horus grants true sight — it might reveal what the stars are hiding.
reveal-star-treasure.solvedText: You press the Eye of Horus into the slot. The obsidian drinks the starlight, then erupts — projecting a web of constellations across the floor in lines of cold fire. Where the beams converge, a hidden compartment splits open in the obsidian, revealing the Scepter of Ra — golden, radiant, humming with the authority of a god.
